title: Sambuca — Modern Boat-Shaped Arched Harp Inspired by the Royal Cemetery of Ur
release_gates: Heifer Zephyr brand integration option locked (V-B-01)

license.rationale: Dual-license. Hardware design (cad/, drawings/, cnc/, BOM and family-spec CSVs) under CERN-OHL-W-2.0 (Weakly Reciprocal) for explicit patent provisions and copyleft on design derivatives. Written content and media (md docs, capstone deck, print packet, photos, site) under CC-BY-4.0 to match the Heifer Zephyr workshop precedent and maximize discoverability.

drawings.status: SVGs deferred until SolidWorks MasterLayout rebuild completes

cnc.status: pre-CAM operation plan; G-code generated by CAM software downstream

site.status: private-review banner; pulls images from images/ when populated

learn_to_play.status: placeholder; downstream sheet-music skill populates after tuning is locked on MULE

harness.status: design notes for SAM-19-MID and SAM-25-CONCERT harnesses

physical_design_anchors.tuning_SAM_13_ROOT: G-major, G3 → E5 (A-major retune documented in supplier-rfq.md)
physical_design_anchors.wood_pairing_SAM_13_ROOT: Walnut hull / Western red cedar soundboard / Sapele neck
physical_design_anchors.string_termination: Glued-on string-holder strip near bow end of soundboard (no separate bridge bar)
physical_design_anchors.soundhole: Single oval keel port, 80 × 50 mm, forward-tilt 15°
physical_design_anchors.playing_position: Seated, long axis between thighs, neck rises away from torso, both hands symmetric

cultural_provenance.pattern: Sumerian, Royal Cemetery at Ur, c. 2600 BC
cultural_provenance.claim: Inspired-by — not a reproduction, not a recreation of any specific reconstructed instrument, not a claim of cultural authority over any modern living tradition
cultural_provenance.sambyke_etymology: Historically contested; build acknowledges the link without claiming it
cultural_provenance.reference_artifact: British Museum object 121198 (b/c), Royal Cemetery at Ur
cultural_provenance.reference_cylinder_seal: Penn Museum object 47880 / B16728 (Pu-abi's tomb, PG 800B)

sources.title: BM 121198 display card and gallery photos
